content: Diagnosis requires colonoscopy with biopsy. Complete colonoscopy essential to rule out synchronous lesions (3-5% have multiple primaries). Pathology: adenocarcinoma histologic grade; lymphovascular/perineural invasion; tumor budding; margin status. Biomarker testing: MMR IHC or PCR for MSI (all patients should be tested); RAS (KRAS/NRAS exons 2-4); BRAF V600E; HER2 for metastatic disease; NTRK fusions (rare). Staging: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis; pelvic MRI for rectal cancer (assesses mesorectal fascia involvement); PET-CT if equivocal findings. CEA baseline for surveillance. TNM staging: T depth of invasion, N lymph nodes (≥12 should be examined), M metastases. Genetic counseling/testing for Lynch syndrome if diagnosed <50 years, MSI-H tumor, strong family history (Amsterdam criteria, Bethesda guidelines). Surveillance post-curative resection: CEA every 3-6 months for 2 years then every 6 months until 5 years; CT annually for 3-5 years; colonoscopy at 1 year, then 3 years, then every 5 years if normal.
